1._: Aderyn comes down off the high deer-paths to the crossroads, hood back and bow cased, moving like someone who would rather not be on a road at all, and stops short at the gather-stone — because the marks freshly scratched into its face are not nothing. She reads them the way she reads a track: recent, deliberate, wrong. Two others stand at the meeting of the ways, come up off other roads. The lights she came to find are above the ridges beyond the settlement; but these marks are here, now.

content._: aderyn's account at Thousand Valleys, beat by beat.
